_comment: agentcarousel golden file — do not edit without bumping bundle_version

evaluator_notes: golden_threshold: 0.70 — allow 30% variation in phrasing and structure
REQUIRED: vulnerability class named (SQL injection / SQLi / injection)
REQUIRED: root cause identified (f-string interpolation / string formatting in query)
REQUIRED: concrete remediation present (parameterized query / prepared statement)
ALLOWED: variation in exact phrasing, severity labeling, or additional context
ALLOWED: additional observations about error handling or return type
FAIL: response that omits the vulnerability name entirely
FAIL: response that recommends input sanitization as the primary remediation without mentioning parameterized queries

response.location: src/db/users.py — f-string interpolation in SQL query
response.description: The function constructs a SQL query using an f-string with unsanitized user input (`email`). An attacker can craft a malicious email value to manipulate the query structure, potentially extracting or modifying arbitrary data.
response.remediation:
Replace the f-string with a parameterized query:

```python
query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = %s"
cursor.execute(query, (email,))
```

This ensures the database driver handles escaping, eliminating the injection vector.
response.additional_notes: Parameterized queries are the only reliable defense against SQL injection — input validation or escaping are insufficient on their own.
